items.title: 24-hour control centre activated after TTDI mud flood incident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Emergency mitigation works and a round-the-clock control centre have been ordered following a mud flood that hit Jalan Haji Openg in Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI) yesterday. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said several immediate measures have been activated to address the crisis. Additional works involve maintenance of all unlined drains at the site, removal of collapsed structures, installation of New Jersey Barriers (NJB), traffic control coordination by the contractor together with the Public Works Department (PWD) and City Hall (DBKL), flushing of drainage systems, as well as ongoing cleaning and maintenance works.

items.title: Malaysian delegation to carry protest voice on Palestine issue in Brussels
items.summary: PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the party has named two representatives, Ledang MP Syed Ibrahim Syed Noh and Balik Pulau MP Datuk Muhammad Bakhtiar Wan Chik, to attend the conference. Amirudin said participation by representatives from various parties, including UMNO and PAS, reflects Malaysia’s firm unity on global humanitarian issues, following the Israeli Parliament’s decision to impose the death penalty without the right of appeal on Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks. On the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 mission, he said the humanitarian aid mission is now approaching Sicily, Italy, after facing delays due to weather conditions.

items.title: GOF nabs smuggler for making repeated petrol trips for RM15 fee
items.summary: TUMPAT: A smuggler who made repeated petrol purchases for a RM15 fee per trip was arrested by the General Operations Force (GOF) at the Pos Nibong shortcut yesterday morning. The 9.50am arrest was the first made by the GOF since the agency was tasked with monitoring nine petrol stations along the Kelantan border. Southeast Brigade GOF commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said Battalion 9 personnel spotted a Mitsubishi Pajero making multiple petrol purchases within a single hour.

items.title: Sim: Leaders must face public criticism, not hide
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Leaders, including those in government, must not shy away from engaging on the ground and facing public criticism, even if it means being scolded by disgruntled citizens. "Today, not only in some countries but also at the federal level, when we become the government, we must listen to criticism from the people. Sim said that DAP itself had long encouraged public scrutiny of the government, including the right of citizens to criticise those in power.

items.title: Nafas to leverage Malaysia-Australia cooperation to secure fertiliser supply chain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The National Farmers Organisation (Nafas) will keep working with the government and industry leaders to leverage Malaysia-Australia cooperation, secure the fertiliser supply chain and boost national food security. Nafas said G2G (government-to-government) cooperation is a significant step toward securing critical raw material for the fertiliser industry amid global supply chain uncertainties and the ongoing energy crisis, which directly impacts agriculture and food production. "As the agency responsible for fertiliser supply to the agricultural sector, Nafas prioritises stable raw materials like CIRP (Christmas Island Rock Phosphate) to ensure continuous and efficient domestic fertiliser production.

items.title: Search launched for five-year-old girl feared snatched by crocodile in Lahad Datu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — A five-year-old girl is missing and feared to have been snatched by a crocodile while playing at a beach near Jalan Felda Sahabat 16, Desa Kencana in Lahad Datu this morning. According to Buletin TV3 , Lahad Datu Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) acting chief Mohd Hamka Mustamin said the department received an emergency call at 10.27am via the MERS999 line. “A team from the Lahad Datu BBP has been deployed to the scene to carry out search and rescue (SAR) operations,” he said when contacted.

items.title: Kg Bahagia fire must be 'turning point' for safer housing, says MP
items.summary: SANDAKAN: The massive fire at Kampung Bahagia must serve as a turning point toward developing more planned and secure settlements, said Batu Sapi member of parliament Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan. Khairul Firdaus said the government has taken a significant step by gazetting the settlement as village land, paving the way for more systematic development. Khairul Firdaus, who is also Deputy Human Resources Minister, said all parties are working hard to ensure aid is channelled effectively to ease the victims' burden.

items.title: Three feared dead after gunman opens fire at food court
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: At least three people are feared dead after a gunman opened fire at a food court in Taman Kota Jaya in a brazen daylight shooting today. Police are at the scene and investigations are underway, he told the New Straits Times. The shooting occurred at 1.45pm at the Medan Selera Taman Kota Jaya food court, an eatery known locally as kedai bulat due to its circular layout.

items.title: Three killed in broad daylight shooting in Kota Tinggi, suspect caught trying to flee
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Three people were killed in a shooting outside a food outlet in Kota Jaya, Kota Tinggi in Johor Bahru, this afternoon. Police detained a man suspected of involvement shortly after the shooting while he was attempting to flee. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad confirmed the incident when contacted, adding that officers had seized a pistol believed to have been used in the attack.

items.title: Zahid: Biofuel shows potential as new alternative energy source
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Malaysia currently has 19 biofuel production plants based on rubber waste, with a monthly capacity of approximately 1.5 million litres. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said, as such, biofuels have the potential to become a new alternative energy source amid the global energy crisis. "There are now 19 biofuel production plants ranging from B15 to B50.

items.title: Illegal racers ride against traffic to evade police cordons
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Riding against the flow of traffic has been identified as one of the tactics used by illegal street racers or mat rempit to evade police cordons during an integrated operation along Jalan Tun Razak early this morning. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief assistant commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the dangerous manoeuvre was detected throughout Op Mabuk and Op Samseng Jalanan conducted from 10pm yesterday until 7am today, involving 102 policemen. The integrated operation also involved personnel from the Road Transport Department (RTD), the National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) and Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL).

items.title: Revive the Bandung spirit for century of peace
items.summary: THE 20th century was the most violent in human history because modern Western culture perfected industrialised slaughter. Two world wars killed tens of millions. But the signature act of Western violence remains the atomic bomb.

items.title: TTDI mud flood: Residents claim warning signs were ignored
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Residents living near Jalan Haji Openg in Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI) claim there were clear warning signs of a possible collapse before yesterday's mud flood at a nearby construction site. Speaking to the New Straits Times at the scene, resident Aniza Anuar, 52, said they had long anticipated such an incident since construction began on the hill. Aniza said land clearing for the National Landscape Department (JLN) project resumed in 2023, involving about six acres (2.4ha) of land.

items.title: Zahid: New ‘green rubber fuel’ could ease rising energy costs
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 19 — Malaysia currently has 19 biofuel production plants based on rubber waste, with a monthly capacity of approximately 1.5 million litres, thus having the potential to become a new alternative energy source amid the global energy crisis. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said that the biofuel production initiative had been presented to the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) through collaboration between government agencies and private companies in the plantation sector. “There are now 19 biofuel production plants ranging from B15 to B50.

items.title: Kampung Bahagia fire: Victims rise to over 9,000
items.summary: District police chief Assistant Commissioner George Abd Rakman said police received a report on the fire at 1.30am, and the area was declared a disaster zone by 4am. "Thankfully, the fire was brought under control by the Fire and Rescue Department," he told reporters at the scene. Earlier, Sandakan Fire and Rescue Station chief Jimmy Lagung said the blaze engulfed an area of more than four hectares.

items.title: RM1.8mil boost for HSNI facilities, RM3.4mil for district clinics – Dzulkefly
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: The Health Ministry has approved a RM1.8 million special allocation to upgrade facilities at Sultanah Nora Ismail Hospital (HSNI) to bolster healthcare delivery for residents in the district. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the funding includes an additional RM800,000 to upgrade the Visitor Centre and improve existing infrastructure and assets. Dzulkefly said a further RM3.421 million has been allocated to upgrade four health clinics in the district: Parit Yaani, Seri Gading, Tongkang Pechah, and Parit Raja.

items.title: Zambry: UM report admits ‘sensitive’ elements in theatre row, corrective steps underway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Universiti Malaya (UM) has acknowledged “sensitive” elements in a recent theatre performance that drew online backlash, submitting a report to the Higher Education Ministry. According to The Star , Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abdul Kadir said the university, in its report, admitted that parts of the performance “were sensitive and required improvement”, adding that steps were already under way to address the issue. Speaking to reporters after a walkabout at the Sabah Zone II Jom Masuk Universiti 2026 programme at Universiti Malaysia Sabah in Kota Kinabalu, Zambry said creative expression must still sit within accepted norms.

items.title: #NSTviral: Penang NRD praised for compassionate response to pregnant woman seeking help
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Penang National Registration Department (NRD) has drawn praise for its compassionate and non-judgmental response to a pregnant woman, in a move seen as a proactive effort to prevent baby dumping. The exchange began on Threads when the woman sought advice on how to safely place her child for adoption after birth. Do not leave the child in a public place," the department replied.

items.title: Stronger prevention, public awareness in high-risk areas to curb corruption - MACC
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Prevention and public awareness must be strengthened alongside enforcement in high-risk areas vulnerable to smuggling, cross-border crime, duty-free abuse, and customs issues, said the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). "To that end, alongside enforcement, we must keep strengthening prevention and public awareness efforts," she said at the opening of the Kedah MACC Langkawi branch office, according to a statement released today. She said Langkawi is not merely a tourist spot but a key national gateway with intense flows of people, goods, and economic activity, noting that the Langkawi MACC office forms part of the national effort to reinforce enforcement integrity and plug loopholes that threaten national interests.

items.title: AGC reviewing tribunal plan to standardise compensation for victims’ families
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — The Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC) is studying a proposal to set up a special tribunal to handle compensation claims for families of crime victims. Attorney General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar said the idea requires detailed scrutiny to ensure it can be implemented effectively. If established, he said, the tribunal should operate within the existing legal framework.

items.title: 'Who is he to deprive us?' Iran fires back at Trump on nuclear issue
items.summary: DUBAI: US President Donald Trump has no justification to deprive Iran of its nuclear rights, the Iranian Student News Agency quoted Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian as saying on Sunday, as Washington and Teheran continue to face disagreements over nuclear issues. "Trump says Iran cannot make use of its nuclear rights but doesn't say for what crime. Pezeshkian was quoted as saying.

items.title: Breaking the silence on mental health at work
items.summary: Yet, one critical element continues to be overlooked or, worse, deliberately ignored — mental health. "Breaking the Silence: Mental Health Matters at Work" is not just a theme; it is a call to action. Mental health is not a personal issue alone, it is a workplace issue.

items.title: Zahid: More big names expected to return to Umno via Rumah Bangsa
items.summary: Umno president and Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said many of these leaders had already met with him. We welcome all parties to join Umno without conditions," he said. Zahid said Umno would simplify the process for those wishing to return through the Rumah Bangsa committee, chaired by vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin.

items.title: Zahid praises national Quran recitation and memorisation champs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi congratulated the champions of the National Quran Recitation and Memorisation Competition 1447H/2026M. The men's title went to Cik Hasnul Taqiyuddin Cik Sazali of Perak, while Sarawakian Nur Syakura Mohamad Musa won the women's title. Cik Hasnul Taqiyuddin, 25, is a third-year student of Quran and Sunnah at Universiti Sains Islam Malaysia, while Nur Syakura, 31, is a Quran teacher supervisor with the Sarawak Islamic Council.

items.title: Police: More than 9,000 displaced after fire destroys 1,000 homes in Sandakan
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 19 — A total of 9,007 residents were affected after about 1,000 stilt houses were destroyed in a fire at Kampung Bahagia here early today. District police chief ACP George Abd Rakman (rpt: Rakman) said police received a report on the incident at 1.30am, before the area was declared a disaster zone at 4 am. “Thankfully, the fire was brought under control by the Fire and Rescue Department,” he told reporters at the scene.

items.title: Police raid three condo-based call centres in Sungai Nibong, arrest 11
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 19 — Police raided three condominiums in Sungai Nibong that were being used as call centres for an online fraud syndicate, arresting 11 individuals, including foreign nationals. “The arrests comprise three Chinese men, two Taiwanese men, two Vietnamese men and one woman, one Japanese man, and two local men, aged between 21 and 56. “Initial investigations found that all suspects were working as customer service agents, operating fake Facebook accounts for online fraud,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Iran war sparks UK-EU reset as Trump ties fray
items.summary: LONDON: Britain's government is set to announce legislation next month to move the country closer to the European Union, as the Iran war sours the UK's so-called special relationship with the United States. President Donald Trump's unpredictability and stream of insults towards America's historic ally is adding impetus to Prime Minister Keir Starmer's bid to deepen ties with the 27-nation bloc, a decade after Britons narrowly voted to leave the EU. Starmer's administration is preparing an EU "reset" bill that will give ministers powers to align UK standards with EU single market rules as they evolve – something called "dynamic alignment."

items.title: MACC urges tighter safeguards to curb smuggling and corruption in high-risk regions
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 19 — Prevention and public awareness must be strengthened alongside enforcement in high-risk areas vulnerable to smuggling, cross-border crime, duty-free abuse, and customs issues, said the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). MACC Deputy Chief Commissioner (Management and Professional) Datuk Junipah Wandi said such issues have implications for national security and the economy. “To that end, alongside enforcement, we must keep strengthening prevention and public awareness efforts,” she said at the opening of the Kedah MACC Langkawi branch office, according to a statement released today.

items.title: Australian soldier charged with war crimes vows to clear his name
items.summary: SYDNEY: An Australian former soldier charged with committing war crimes in Afghanistan vowed on Sunday to clear his name, saying he had never "run from a fight" in his first public comments since his arrest. Roberts-Smith was granted bail on Friday after a high-profile arrest on five counts of "war crime – murder." "I'm proud of my service in Afghanistan.

items.title: Sarawak rejects ‘golden goose’ accusation, says gas push is about fairness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Sarawak will pursue its own framework to manage the state’s gas resources without affecting the role of Petroliam Nasional Berhad (Petronas), Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Abang Openg said today. According to Berita Harian , he said the formula being developed is not intended to “kill” the national oil company, but to ensure fairness for Sarawak as a major gas producer. “This is what we want to implement in gas management,” he said at the Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) convention at the Borneo Convention Centre Kuching (BCCK) in Kuching.

items.title: Trader cheated of RM25,270 in QR code Harumanis scam
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A trader suffered losses amounting to RM25,280 after being duped by a syndicate in an online Harumanis mango sale scam using a quick response (QR) code. District police chief assistant commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the 53-year-old victim lodged a report at 7.41pm yesterday after realising unauthorised withdrawals had been made from his bank account. "After scanning the code, the victim's mobile phone became unresponsive for about 20 minutes," he said in a statement.

items.title: At Cuba's Bay of Pigs, a victory against US invaders stirs pride
items.summary: ORESTES Chamizo points at a scrubby patch of sand on Cuba's Bay of Pigs, showing the spot where a brigade of US-trained Cuban exiles landed on the island's southern coast on April 17, 1961. The prospect of communist-run Cuba resisting a military invasion by the global superpower to the north appears as improbable today as it did 65 years ago. Cuba's constitution requires the country's youth to undergo military training.

items.title: Police raid three condo-based call centres in Sungai Nibong, arrest 11
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police raided three condominiums in Sungai Nibong that were being used as call centres for an online fraud syndicate, arresting 11 people, including foreigners. "The arrests comprise three Chinese men, two Taiwanese men, two Vietnamese men and one woman, one Japanese man, and two local men, aged between 21 and 56. "Initial investigations found that all suspects were working as customer service agents, operating fake Facebook accounts for online fraud," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Muhyiddin says emergency call shows fear of losing polls
items.summary: GOMBAK: Calls to declare an emergency to postpone the Melaka and Johor state elections are a sign that the government is afraid of facing defeat, says Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin. "There is no reason to declare an emergency. A DAP representative has requested it to postpone the Melaka and Johor elections.

items.title: DAP must reassess direction to stay relevant, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, April 19 — DAP must reassess its direction and set a clear strategic path to ensure the continuity of its struggle, said secretary-general Anthony Loke Siew Fook. He said the party’s national convention, scheduled to be held in the next two to three months, will serve as a key platform for leaders and members to reflect, deliberate and determine the party’s future direction. That spirit is what has kept the party strong until today,” he said.

items.title: DAP must press on with long-term 'Malaysian Dream', says Lim Kit Siang
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: DAP stalwart Tan Sri Lim Kit Siang has called on the party to remain committed to a long-term vision of a "Malaysian Dream" anchored on unity, reform and inclusivity, as it celebrates its 60th anniversary. Speaking at the party's congress, Lim reflected on DAP's evolution from a small political outfit in the 1960s to a major national party with a broad organisational base. Lim said DAP had grown from just seven branches in 1966 to about 2,200 branches today, with membership expanding to an estimated 200,000.

items.title: Iran pulls out of DSA 2026 over logistics issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Iran has withdrawn from the Defence Services Asia (DSA) and National Security (Natsec) Asia 2026 exhibitions at the eleventh hour due to logistical challenges stemming from the conflict in the Middle East. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the withdrawal was not politically motivated but resulted from operational difficulties involving the movement and delivery of assets. He said this at a press conference after inspecting final preparations for the exhibition at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre (Mitec) here today.

items.title: Leave PKR if you no longer align with its struggle, Amirudin tells disgruntled members
items.summary: GOMBAK: PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said any member or leader who no longer agrees with the party's direction is free to leave. He said this when asked about Pandan member of parliament Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, who is alleged to have gone against party decisions on several key issues. Amirudin, who is also Gombak PKR division chief, said his remarks were intended to ensure all leaders and members remained focused on the party's mandate without disrupting internal stability.

items.title: Spoilt deal: Terengganu trader loses RM25,000 in Harumanis mango QR scam
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, April 19 — A trader suffered losses amounting to RM25,280 after being duped by a syndicate in an online Harumanis mango sale scam using a quick response (QR) code. Kuala Terengganu District Police Chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said the 53-year-old victim lodged a report at 7.41pm yesterday after realising unauthorised withdrawals had been made from his bank account. After scanning the code, the victim’s mobile phone became unresponsive for about 20 minutes,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Nga: DAP's track record key to winning next general election
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: DAP's proven track record in delivering on its promises will be a key factor as the country heads towards the next general election, said its national deputy chairman Nga Kor Ming. He said the party's credibility, built over decades of struggle and strengthened through tangible results in government, would be central to winning voters' trust. Highlighting the party's achievements in government, Nga said DAP had fulfilled key reform promises, including strengthening Parliament through the Parliamentary Services Act and introducing Prime Minister's Question Time.

items.title: Zahid: 6,252 former members apply to rejoin Umno in sign of resurgence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — More than 6,200 former Umno members have applied to rejoin the party, its president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today, hailing the surge as a boost for the party. This comes as Umno said it had approved the unconditional return of former Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin, former vice president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and former foreign minister Tan Sri Syed Hamid Albar. On efforts to attract younger members, Ahmad Zahid said Umno has introduced a fully online application system to replace what he described as a cumbersome, multi-layered process.

items.title: DAP must remain rakyat's voice in government or risk losing relevance
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: DAP must continue to act as the voice of the people in government or risk losing its relevance and public trust, DAP national chairman Gobind Singh Deo said. He said the party's role in the federal administration goes beyond holding office, stressing that it must consistently raise rakyat concerns and push for reforms at the highest level. "The voice of the people must reach the Cabinet.

items.title: Melaka Straits secure, not comparable to Strait of Hormuz, says Khaled Nordin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin has assured that security and stability in the Straits of Melaka remain intact, dismissing concerns that it could face risks similar to the Strait of Hormuz. He said this at a press conference after inspecting final preparations for the Defence Security Asia (DSA) 2026 exhibition at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre (Mitec) here today. On a related matter, Khaled said current global conflicts have provided lessons on the changing landscape of modern warfare.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh: Urgent interventions underway after TTDI retaining wall collapse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — City authorities have shifted into full crisis-response mode, mobilising additional manpower, machinery and round-the-clock monitoring to contain and repair damage at a project site, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said today. Yeoh also said she had requested immediate support from the Public Works Department (JKR) headquarters, with Selangor JKR also stepping in to deploy extra machinery to speed up repair works. Authorities have also moved to clear collapsed structures and install barriers, while traffic control is being coordinated jointly by the contractor, JKR and Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL).

items.title: Is Perlis govt on shaky ground? Analysts say delayed sitting raises red flags
items.summary: KANGAR, April 19 — The sudden postponement of the Perlis State Legislative Assembly (DUN) sitting, offered without a solid reason, could quietly feed a dangerous perception: that the state government is either grappling with political instability or buckling under internal pressure, political analysts warn. “In politics, when a DUN sitting is postponed without a clear explanation, the public reads between the lines. This sitting was supposed to be a milestone: the first DUN session under the leadership of Abu Bakar Hamzah, after the state Bersatu chief was sworn in as Perlis Menteri Besar on Dec 28 last year, a new chapter for a state watching anxiously.

items.title: Foster family under police remand till April 25 after Kuala Terengganu toddler dies from injuries
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, April 19 — The remand of three foster family members suspected of abusing a three-year-old boy to death in Kampung Banggol Katong has been extended for six days, beginning tomorrow. Kuala Terengganu police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said the remand order, issued by the Kuala Terengganu Magistrate’s Court this morning, will run until April 25 after the previous remand period ended today. The remand order against all suspects has been extended for six days until April 25,” he said when contacted.

items.title: Hundreds of ‘mat rempit’ caught in pre-dawn dragnet on Jalan Tun Razak hotspot
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Hundreds of mat rempit (illegal street racers) were caught in a pre-dawn police dragnet on Jalan Tun Razak here, a hotspot for high-risk riding and stunts. Jalan Tun Razak has become a draw for youths from across the Klang Valley, many showcasing heavily modified motorcycles fitted with loud exhaust systems. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said a large-scale operation was carried out under Op Mabuk, Op Motosikal, Op Tutup and Op Samseng Jalanan across the capital from 10pm yesterday to 7am today.

items.title: Afternoon storm alert: MetMalaysia warns of rain and lightning in KL, Putrajaya and six states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and six other states until 3 pm today. In a statement today, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the affected states include Pahang, covering Cameron Highlands, Bentong, Temerloh, Bera and Rompin, and Selangor, involving Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang. In Sarawak, similar conditions are expected in Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Sri Aman (Lubok Antu) and Kapit (Song and Kapit).

items.title: Cooking mishap blamed for Sandakan fire leaving at least 8,000 homeless
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 19 — A cooking incident allegedly caused by negligence has led to a major fire that displaced about 8,000 residents in Kampung Bahagia, Batu Sapi early Sunday morning. Village head Sharif Hashim Sharif Iting said the blaze began around 1.30am in a house at Bridge A after a resident was unable to control a fire from a gas stove while cooking. Despite efforts by nearby residents to extinguish the flames before firefighters arrived, the fire spread rapidly.

items.title: Iran absence at KL defence show down to logistics, not politics, says Khaled
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Companies from Iran pulled out at the last minute from participating in this year’s Defence Services Asia (DSA) and National Security (Natsec) Asia 2026 exhibition, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said today. He said the withdrawal came despite earlier indications that Iranian firms were prepared to take part in the biennial defence showcase. But this time, companies from Iran have withdrawn,” he said during a press conference after a walkabout at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre (Mitec) here.

items.title: Batu Pahat hospital gets RM1.8m to upgrade patient services
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, April 19 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has approved a special allocation of RM1.8 million to upgrade facilities at Sultanah Nora Ismail Hospital (HSNI) in an effort to strengthen healthcare service delivery for residents in the Batu Pahat district. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the allocation includes an additional RM800,000 on top of the original amount, with a focus on upgrading the Visitor Centre, as well as improving existing infrastructure and assets at the hospital. In addition, Dr Dzulkefly said RM3.421 million has been allocated to upgrade four health clinics in the district, namely the Parit Yaani Health Clinic, Seri Gading Health Clinic, Tongkang Pechah Health Clinic and Parit Raja Health Clinic, to ensure a more equitable distribution of healthcare resources.

items.title: Defence minister defends DSA-Natsec Asia 2026, says arms expo is neutral trade platform
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin today defended the participation of foreign defence firms at the Defence Services Asia (DSA) and National Security (Natsec) Asia 2026 exhibition, describing it as a neutral trade platform despite concerns over alleged links to conflicts abroad. Speaking after a walkabout at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre (Mitec) here, Khaled said such criticisms were not new and had surfaced during previous editions of the biennial event. Khaled said the platform also allows Malaysia to assess and compare defence technologies as part of its procurement considerations.

items.title: No parallel with Hormuz situation in Malacca Strait, says defence minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Tensions in the Middle East, particularly in the Strait of Hormuz, will not be mirrored in the Strait of Malacca, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said today. Khaled said comparisons between the two chokepoints were misplaced, noting that countries bordering the Strait of Malacca — Malaysia, Indonesia and Singapore — do not face the same geopolitical pressures as Iran. “Malaysia, Indonesia, Singapore, we are countries that abide by the rule of law, international law,” he said.

items.title: Amirudin signals intent to defend Gombak seat in GE16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari has declared he is ready to defend the Gombak parliamentary seat in the next general election, pending a decision by party leadership. “I will defend Gombak Parliament,” he said at the PKR Gombak division annual general meeting today, as reported by Berita Harian . Amirudin said the government is now focused on resolving long-standing public issues under the Madani agenda.

items.title: PM orders urgent govt aid after fire guts 200 homes in Sandakan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — The federal government and the Sabah state government are coordinating basic assistance and temporary accommodation as soon as possible for the victims of the fire in Kampung Bahagia, Sandakan. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he was shocked when he received reports of the fire in the village, which had destroyed hundreds of houses. About 200 houses were destroyed in the fire in the floating village early today.

items.title: PKR leaders cautioned against complacency in power, says Fahmi at Gombak meet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — PKR leaders have been warned against complacency in power, with party information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil urging members not to lose sight of PKR’s reformist roots. According to Berita Harian , Fahmi said that despite leading Selangor since 2008, leaders at all levels — from branch committee members to the top leadership — must remain grounded and united. He stressed that party leaders are under constant public scrutiny.

items.title: ‘I can’t wait’: Teen defies memory loss to reclaim UK future after Help University campus blast
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — A teenager rebuilding her life after a devastating campus explosion is setting her sights on a return abroad, framing her recovery with the kind of resolve more often seen on the sporting stage. A month after regaining consciousness, 19-year-old Reeya Kaur Thandal still cannot recall the moment an air-conditioning compressor exploded at Help University in Kuala Lumpur — an incident that left her with skull fractures and bleeding in the brain. “I read messages from a WhatsApp group where someone said it was something like an explosion in my class.

items.title: Malbatt troops safe but Malaysia condemns deadly attack on UN mission in Lebanon
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 19 — Malaysia has called on the international community and the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) to take appropriate measures to ensure the safety and security of the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) peacekeepers. “We urge all parties to respect the ceasefire, de-escalate tensions, and extend full cooperation to Unifil in the discharge of its mandate under United Nations Security Council Resolution 1701 (2006). “The Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) currently deployed under Unifil is reported to be safe,” the statement read.

items.title: Bukit Aman: Two policemen among 87 testing positive for drugs in Ipoh entertainment outlet raids
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Two policemen tested positive for drugs after being detained in raids on entertainment outlets in Ipoh, where 87 patrons were also found to have taken illicit substances. Bukit Aman’s Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (JSJN) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the April 5 raids targeted two premises in Bandar Baru Medan Ipoh and Taman Ipoh. “Checks at the second premises found that 87 patrons, aged between 19 and 52, tested positive for drugs,” he said, as reported by Sinar Harian today.

items.title: Loke orders probe after KLIA baggage chaos, says standards ‘clearly failed’ (VIDEO)
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 19 — The government has ordered an investigation into a baggage handling breakdown at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1, with Transport Minister Anthony Loke warning that lapses at the country’s main gateway will not be tolerated. In a statement today, Loke said the disruption yesterday caused baggage delays of between two and four hours for a “significant number” of arriving passengers, adding that the inconvenience was “deeply regretted”. “Passengers who travel through our national gateway deserve a standard of service reliability that this incident has clearly failed to meet,” he said.
